attacking_style: Demonstrates willingness to launch long-range solo efforts (35km out) and bridge attempts on late-race climbs. Prefers TT-style power moves rather than explosive accelerations, often going too early.
team_tactics: Frequently deployed as domestique but given freedom to finish when work is done and sprint trains disrupted. Limited leadout support in sprints, forced to self-position through chaotic finales.
terrain_preference: Excels on punchy late-race climbs (Côte du Pondoff) and rolling courses where power sustains bridges. Exposed in pure sprints lacking organized leadout trains.
vulnerability: Overcommits on early long-range attacks that fail (35km solo caught). Perceived as sprint threat, deliberately dropped by breakaway companions. Lacks positioning support in organized bunch sprints.
key_patterns: Launches solo bridge attempts to front groups on late climbs (2019, 2020 evidence).
Used as protective domestique, opportunistically finishes when sprint chaos allows (2020, implied 2019).
Targets disrupted sprint scenarios lacking organized trains, struggles in pure leadout finishes (2018, 2020).
Makes ambitious early solo moves beyond sustainable range, caught by organized chasers (2017, 2020).
Rivals eliminate him from breakaways pre-finish, fearing sprint capacity (2019 tactical exclusion).
form_trajectory: neutral: showed dangerous bridging ability in 2020 but remains tactically inconsistent, alternating domestique duties with opportunistic finishes
